# Global Conversation History

Scope: [project collection, workspace, organization, or directory tree]
Related local file: `history-chat.md` in each specific project

## Usage Guideline

Use this file to record reusable lessons across projects. It does not replace each project's local `history-chat.md`.

Record here:

- reusable technical or editorial patterns;
- workflows that save time in future projects;
- useful validations, checklists, and commands;
- recurring errors and ways to prevent them;
- organization decisions that apply to multiple folders/projects;
- scripts, conventions, and cautions with value beyond the current project.

Avoid here:

- circumstantial details from one task;
- long lists of specific files without reusable value;
- sensitive data, secrets, tokens, passwords, or unnecessary private information;
- duplication of the local project history.

## YYYY-MM-DD - [Reusable Topic]

- Reusable context: [when this lesson applies]
- Recommended practice: [what to do]
- Useful validations: [commands, checklists, or criteria]
- Risks avoided: [errors this prevents]
- Applies to: [project types, folders, or situations]

## YYYY-MM-DD - [Reusable Flow or Checklist]

- Objective: [expected result]
- Recommended flow:
  - [step 1]
  - [step 2]
  - [step 3]
- Evidence of completion: [how to know it is done]
- When to update this lesson: [conditions]

## Update Criteria

- Update when a session produces a useful method, important prevention, recurring error fix, reusable checklist, or organization decision that may help other projects.
- Keep the text summarized, dated, and reuse-oriented.
- If the content is project-specific, record it in the local `history-chat.md` and summarize here only the reusable part.
